growth_records.strain: not stated (grown as per Usai et al., 2020; strain not specified in text)
growth_records.medium: D-glucose defined medium (per Usai et al., 2020), control (no butanol)

up.method: glucose oxidase/peroxidase on cell-free supernatant + HPLC; measured over first 4 h

sec.method: HPLC (5 mM H2SO4, 0.5 mL/min, 50C; UV 210 nm / RI)
HPLC; titer computed from reported acetate yield x glucose consumed
HPLC; titer computed from reported butyrate yield x glucose consumed

growth_records.cite: Costa, Paolo et al. Clostridium cellulovorans Proteomic Responses to Butanol Stress. Frontiers in Microbiology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2021.674639. [PMC8336468]
growth_records.method: reported (OD600 hourly; specific growth rate mu shown only graphically in Figure 1B, no numeric value in text)
growth_records.snippet: Almost a double amount (0.84 +/- 0.18 g/L) of glucose was consumed by C. cellulovorans grown in control condition... formate titer significantly decreased (from 0.30 g/L to 0.07 g/L)... only 0.13 g/g acetate and 0.43 g/g butyrate were produ
growth_records.notes: CONTROL (no butanol) condition. Batch, anaerobic, 37C, 200 rpm, D-glucose 5 g/L (full medium composition referenced to Usai et al. 2020, not restated -> medium_composition_stated:false). Glucose consumed over first 4 h = 0.84 +/- 0.18 g/L; volumetric qS = 0.84/4 = 0.21 g/L/h. Per-gDW specific rate NOT derivable: absolute biomass not given numerically (biomass = protein/0.261 conversion factor, but no protein g reported in text; only 'max biomass ~50% of control at 6 g/L butanol'). Product yields reported directly (g product / g glucose consumed): formate 0.33, acetate 0.13, butyrate 0.43. Derived secretion titers = yield x 0.84 g/L glucose: acetate 0.13x0.84=0.109 g/L, butyrate 0.43x0.84=0.361 g/L (formate titer 0.30 g/L reported directly; 0.33x0.84=0.277 g/L, consistent). Lactate not detected (LDH minor flux). Ethanol detected, no numeric value, no significant difference vs butanol condition. mu shown only in Figure 1B graphically; no numeric growth rate in accessible text. pH not stated.

growth_records.strain: not stated (grown as per Usai et al., 2020; strain not specified in text)
growth_records.medium: D-glucose defined medium supplemented with 6 g/L butanol

up.method: glucose oxidase/peroxidase on cell-free supernatant + HPLC; measured over first 4 h

sec.method: HPLC (5 mM H2SO4, 0.5 mL/min, 50C; UV 210 nm / RI)
HPLC; titer computed from reported acetate yield x glucose consumed
HPLC; titer computed from reported butyrate yield x glucose consumed

growth_records.cite: Costa, Paolo et al. Clostridium cellulovorans Proteomic Responses to Butanol Stress. Frontiers in Microbiology (2021). DOI:10.3389/fmicb.2021.674639. [PMC8336468]
growth_records.method: reported (OD600 hourly; specific growth rate mu shown only graphically in Figure 1B, significantly reduced vs control; no numeric value in text)
growth_records.snippet: 6 g/L butanol-enriched cultures (0.46 +/- 0.03 g/L glucose consumed)... formate yield (from 0.33 g/g glucose consumed to 0.15 g/g glucose consumed)... 0.29 g/g acetate and 0.62 g/g butyrate were measured in butanol-supplemented condition. N
growth_records.notes: 6 g/L BUTANOL stress condition. Batch, anaerobic, 37C, 200 rpm, D-glucose 5 g/L, 6 g/L n-butanol added (medium comp referenced to Usai et al. 2020, not restated -> medium_composition_stated:false). Glucose consumed over first 4 h = 0.46 +/- 0.03 g/L; volumetric qS = 0.46/4 = 0.115 g/L/h. Paper notes 'specific glucose consumption about 2-fold higher than control cultures' (per-biomass, because biomass ~50% of control) but no absolute per-gDW value given -> not derivable. Product yields reported directly (g/g glucose): formate 0.15, acetate 0.29, butyrate 0.62. Derived secretion titers = yield x 0.46 g/L glucose: acetate 0.29x0.46=0.133 g/L, butyrate 0.62x0.46=0.285 g/L (formate titer 0.07 g/L reported directly; 0.15x0.46=0.069, consistent). Butanol NOT consumed (<0.35 g/L). Lactate not detected. Ethanol no significant change. mu graphical only (Fig 1B), significantly reduced vs control; no numeric value. pH not stated. Max biomass ~50% of control.
